senses.definition: used for saying that something will happen in a very near future

examples.x: The deadline for the project is just around the corner, so we need to work diligently to complete it on time.
She always has a smile on her face because she believes that happiness is just around the corner.
Last week, we were on the verge of bankruptcy, but a major investor came in just when it seemed like failure was around the corner.
Next month, a new opportunity is around the corner, and I'm eager to see what it brings.
The storm clouds are gathering; it looks like heavy rain is just around the corner.
She was on the verge of tears because she had practiced for months, and the dance performance was just around the corner, but she injured her ankle.

senses.definition: used to refer to an upcoming event that will happen within a short time

examples.x: The team is working tirelessly to meet the deadline; completion is at hand.
By this time tomorrow, the final preparations will be underway, and success will be at hand.
The meeting is at hand, so we should prepare.
With the deadline at hand, the team worked late into the night.
With the test at hand, she reviewed her notes one last time.

senses.definition: very near to something in terms of time

examples.x: Students are studying hard in the shadow of the upcoming exams.
The athletes trained tirelessly in the shadow of the upcoming championship, hoping to secure victory.
The company worked diligently in the shadow of the product launch, aiming for a successful debut.
Families make travel plans in the shadow of summer vacation, eagerly awaiting a break from their routines.

word_list.pronunciation: 톩n 톖틣톷 칝t n틛o툵 t틛a톩m 칝t 틛톖틣l 톖틣톷 fl틛칝t 톖틣톷

senses.definition: used when something is done very soon or very fast

examples.x: We had the cake baked and decorated in no time for the party.
Things would move so much faster if everyone pitched in to solve this in no time.
With my new tools, I can change the oil in no time flat.
We packed up the car and left for home in no time flat after the party ended.
I can pack this suitcase in no time at all once I start sorting clothes.
The kids devoured their snacks and finished their homework in no time at all after school.

senses.definition: used to describe something that probably happens in near future

examples.x: She probably knew that something was in the wind but chose not to enquire too closely about it.
There are some major changes in the wind.
There is something in the wind.
With the company doing so poorly these days, we're all afraid that layoffs are in the wind.
She finishes tasks in a New York minute, always ahead of schedule."
He completed the project in a New York minute, impressing his colleagues.
They will have to make a decision in a New York minute, as time is of the essence.
They are working at a frantic pace, trying to get things done in a New York minute.
The team was racing against the clock, working in a New York minute to meet the deadline.

senses.definition: used for saying that probably something will happen or will be done in a short time

examples.x: Exciting new technological advancements are on the horizon, revolutionizing the way we live.
Last year, a major economic recession was on the horizon, causing widespread concern.
In the coming months, promising opportunities for growth and expansion will be on the horizon.
She's been working hard because she knows a promotion is on the horizon.
Throughout the year, changes in the industry were on the horizon, prompting companies to adapt.
By this time next year, groundbreaking discoveries will still be on the horizon in the field of medicine.

senses.definition: at a later point in time or in the future

examples.x: She hopes to pursue further education down the line, once she gains more work experience.
They are considering expanding their business down the line, but for now, they want to focus on current operations.
He regretted not saving enough money earlier, as it would have provided financial security down the line.
They had made strategic investments in technology, which paid off down the line with increased efficiency.
By next year, she will have completed her training, and down the line, she aims to take on a leadership role.
They will start saving for retirement now, knowing that it will provide a comfortable lifestyle down the line.
If they invest wisely, they can secure a stable financial future down the line.
She would have achieved her dreams if she had made certain sacrifices down the line.
Over the years, he has made consistent efforts to improve his skills, knowing it will pay off down the line.
By the time they retired, they had saved enough money to enjoy a comfortable life down the line.
